snippet.title: Why Harriet Oleson Wasn’t in the Last Episode of Little House on the Prairie
snippet.description:
Did you know that the actress that played Harriet Oleson on Little House on the Prairie practiced the Hindu faith and that she went on a pilgrimage to India? Harriet Oleson was a lovable antagonist that appeared throughout all nine seasons of Little House on the Prairie. Despite this fact, the character didn’t show up in the final few episodes of the series, which include the show’s finale. Many fans have since wondered why this was the case, and we now know the answer! It had to do with the burgeoning spiritualism of the actress that played the character.

▬Contents of this video▬
00:00 - Intro
00:23 - Everyone Loved to Hate Harriet Oleson
01:46 - Katherine MacGregor’s Advice Was Mostly Appreciated
03:13 - The Reason for Harriet Oleson’s Absence
04:31 - Katherine Helped Expand Upon Her Character
05:37 - Katherine Retired After Playing Harriet Oleson
06:43 - Outro

Like this content? Subscribe here: https://www.youtube.com/factsverse?sub_confirmation=1 

Or, watch more videos here: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLkXAntdjbcSJlJnpP4FgdU0swKbnkNgJj

Prior to playing Harriet Oleson on Little House on the Prairie, actress Katherine MacGregor worked predominantly on the stage. Though she was relatively new to television, she was still one of the more experienced performers on the set of the show. Actors often turned to her for advice due to her veteran status, though you typically didn’t need to ask in order to receive her critiques. For the most part, people appreciated Katherine’s advice when it was offered. However, others wanted her to mind her own business.

Katherine may have had some issues with her coworkers during the filming of Little House on the Prairie, but she got along with them swimmingly most of the time. The reason that Katherine’s character of Harriet Oleson didn’t appear in the show’s final few episodes had nothing to do with the trouble that the performer was experiencing on set. Instead, it had to do with the trouble she was experiencing in her personal life.

Over the course of the nine seasons that Katherine portrayed Harriet on Little House on the Prairie, the actress was suffering from worsening alcoholism. During the final season, the actress was finally getting help in the form of her burgeoning Hindu faith. This new faith ended up getting in the way of some important filming dates for the show. Join Facts Verse as we explore why Harriet Oleson wasn’t in the final episode of Little House on the Prairie.

Why Harriet Oleson Wasn’t in the Last Episode of Little House on the Prairie

snippet.title: Karen Grassle's Difficult Relationship with Michael Landon
snippet.description:
Hear more from Karen Grassle in her memoir, Bright Lights, Prairie Dust: https://amzn.to/3o6RmkM

Karen Grassle, the beloved actress who played Ma on Little House on the Prairie, grew up at the edge of the Pacific Ocean in a family where love was plentiful but alcohol wreaked havoc. 

In this candid memoir, Grassle reveals her journey to succeed as an actress even as she struggles to overcome depression, combat her own dependence on alcohol, and find true love. With humor and hard-won wisdom, Grassle takes listeners on an inspiring journey through the political turmoil on '60s campuses, on to studies with some of the most celebrated artists at the famed London Academy of Music and Dramatic Arts, and ultimately behind the curtains of Broadway stages and storied Hollywood sets. In these chapters, listeners meet actors and directors who have captivated us on screen and stage as they fall in love, betray and befriend, and don costumes only to reveal themselves. 

We know Karen Grassle best as the proud prairie woman Caroline Ingalls, with her quiet strength and devotion to family, but this memoir introduces listeners to the complex, funny, rebellious, and soulful woman who, in addition to being the force behind those many strong women she played, fought passionately - as a writer, producer, and activist - on behalf of equal rights for women. Raw, emotional, and tender, Bright Lights celebrates and honors womanhood, in all its complexity.

NOTE: As an Amazon Associate I earn from qualifying purchases.
tags: little house on the prairie then and now
little house on the prairie episode
little house on the prairie pilot
little house on the prairie episodes

snippet.title: Ask Me Anything
snippet.description:
En un encuentro cercano con Catherine MacGregor, CEO de ENGIE Group; Pierre-Francois Riolacci, CFO de ENGIE Group; y Axel Leveque, CEO de ENGIE Chile, se llevó a cabo el #AskMeAnything. 

En este evento, que reunió de manera presencial a más de 100 colaboradores y a casi 200 de manera virtual, se conversaron abiertamente diferentes temas de interés general: oportunidades de crecimiento, desafíos en el contexto actual, el desarrollo de las diferentes unidades de negocio, entre otros temas.

localized.description:

En este evento, que reunió de manera presencial a más de 100 colaboradores y a casi 200 de manera virtual, se conversaron abiertamente diferentes temas de interés general: oportunidades de crecimiento, desafíos en el contexto actual, el desarrollo de las diferentes unidades de negocio, entre otros temas.

snippet.title: It's With Our Deepest Regret to Report About Sudden Death Of 'Little House' star Katherine MacGregor
snippet.description:
Katherine MacGregor was an American actress, best known for her role as Harriet Oleson in Little House on the Prairie. She started her career on stage in New York City, in theatres off and on Broadway, credited as Scottie MacGregor.  It's With Our Deepest Regret to Report About Sudden Death Of 'Little House' star Katherine MacGregor
 #LittleHouseonthePrairie
Subscribe for the latest news and politics update. Thanks.

snippet.title: E for Excellence : Round table
snippet.description:
Round table 1 : Our common Goal, Your Own Path with Marie- Ange Debon (CEO of Keolis), Patrick Koller (CEO of Faurecia), Catherine MacGregor (CEO of ENGIE), Anne Rigail (CEO of Air France). 

1:03:08 
Round table 2 : What levers for scaling up ? with Christine Cabau-Woehrel (EVP Assets & operations, CMA CGM), Sylvain Montcouquiol (EVP Sustainability, Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield), Emmanuel Normant (EVP Sustainability, SAINT GOBAIN), Cécile Prévieu (EVP in charge of the Energy Solutions activities, ENGIE).

snippet.title: Commission d'enquête sur l'énergie : Audition de Catherine MacGregor, DG d'Engie
snippet.description:
Commission d'enquête sur la souveraineté et l'indépendance énergétique de la France - 6 décembre 2022

Audition de Mme Catherine MacGregor, directrice générale du groupe Engie.

Retrouvez la réponse à ma question et l'ensemble de la rediffusion ici : @2589032_638f56752774f.souverainete-et-independance-energetique-de-la-france--mme-catherine-macgregor-directrice-generale-6-decembre-2022.

snippet.title: Extrait 5 - Purpose Info Episode 15 avec Catherine MacGregor et Laurent Berger
snippet.description:
[🅿🆄🆁🅿🅾🆂🅴 🅸🅽🅵🅾 - 🅴🆇🆃🆁🅰🅸🆃 5 - 🅴🅿 15]

L'actualité a beaucoup évolué en quelques semaines... Mais c'est un plaisir de se replonger dans ce dernier extrait du brillant débat entre Catherine Fiamma MacGregor (ENGIE) et Laurent Berger (CFDT) !

🎙 Quelles sont vos convictions et expériences en matières d'innovations managériales et de nouveaux schémas organisationnels ?

« C'est d'arriver à avoir à la fois, une organisation qui permet l'excellence opérationnelle et industrielle, [...] et à la fois garder cet ancrage local qui est tellement important. [...] L'idée c'est d'avoir une organisation qui nous permet d'avoir ces deux dimensions qui soient bien représentées. Je me méfie un peu de l'innovation à tout va. Je crois beaucoup en fait au bon sens commun quand il s'agit du management et des organisations. Je pense que des choses comme la proximité avec ses collaborateurs, un dialogue social efficace, un certain courage managérial... Je pense que c'est aussi très important de dire les choses. Ce sont de bons fondamentaux. » C. MacGregor

« Je pense que demain, le management, il sera un peu comme il devrait être depuis toujours. C'est à dire avec de la confiance, avec de la bienveillance (ça n'enlève pas l'exigence !), avec de l'écoute, avec des volontés de coopération. On s'est aperçu que finalement, le travail ce n'était pas simplement l'accomplissement d'un acte individuel de production, quelle qu'elle soit. C'est aussi de l'intelligence collective, de la compétence collective. Et donc la coopération devrait être mise sur le haut de la pile des préoccupations managériales. » L. Berger

#innovationManageriale #Raisondetre #PurposeInfo #Management #LoiPacte 
#Entreprisesàmission #PurposeWashing #Culturedentreprise #RSE #Externalités #CreationdeValeur #Finalités

snippet.title: IFF Paris 2022 - Plenary Session 1 - The Sustainability Revolution
snippet.description:
Valérie BAUDSON, Chief Executive Officer, Amundi
Antoine GOSSET-GRAINVILLE, Chairman, AXA Group
Catherine MacGREGOR, Chief Executive Officer, Engie
Frédéric OUDEA, Chief Executive Officer, Société Générale

Moderator: Alan KATZ, Paris Bureau Chief, Bloomberg
